candidates.name: Mike Dunleavy
candidates.notes: [2025-26 actions: TERM-LIMITED in 2026 — cannot seek re-election to third consecutive term per AK Constitution. AK race wide open.]

profile.education: Misericordia University (BA History 1983), University of Alaska Fairbanks (MEd Education)
profile.background: Spent nearly two decades as teacher, principal, and superintendent in northwest Arctic communities; served in Alaska Senate before election as 12th governor in 2018.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — Default-flagged as incumbent_seeking_reelection (statewide exec, 2026 cycle).

claims.text: Sponsored SB100 creating correspondence school allotments for homeschool families (2013-2014), allowing state funds for educational materials from public/private/religious organizations

candidates.name: Treg Taylor
candidates.notes: [STATUS: RESIGNED as AK AG August 29, 2025. Filed for 2026 AK GOVERNOR race (10th R to enter). During tenure: oversaw "statehood defense" federal litigation; used state homeschool funds for own kids' Christian school tuition; blocked abortion medications in AK pharmacy chains; halted HRC LGBTQ+ rights complaints review.]

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-21 — v5.0 new-category seed (conservative lean inferred from existing God First pattern; party-default archetype, not individual evidence).

notes.trump_impeachment: One of 7 GOP senators who voted to convict Trump in second impeachment trial (2021). Alaska GOP censured her for this vote
notes.abortion: One of few Republican senators who supports abortion rights
notes.marriage: Voted for Respect for Marriage Act (supporting same-sex marriage codification)
notes.moderate: Described as one of Senate's most moderate Republicans
notes.education: Staunch defender of public education, former PTA president

profile.education: Georgetown University (BA Economics 1980), Willamette College of Law (JD 1985)
profile.background: Longest-serving member of Alaska's congressional delegation. Former Alaska state representative. Appointed to Senate in 2002 by her father, Governor Frank Murkowski. Won 2010 write-in campaign.
profile.office: 522 Hart Senate Office Building

aipac.note: TrackAIPAC career-total report shows $265,262 from AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby PACs.
aipac.descriptor: AIPAC operates a super-PAC (United Democracy Project) that spends millions per cycle on US races on behalf of a foreign government's policy agenda. RESOLUTE Citizen treats documented contributions as a failure on america_first/q3.

profile.confidence_note: Top-50 federal rescore pass — establishment_r archetype with individual overrides. candidates.name: Nick Begich III
candidates.notes: U.S. Rep AK-AL (R), freshman 119th Congress (sworn Jan 3, 2025). [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 119th lifetime 92% — orthodox RSC/conservative House line. Hard-evidence wins: original co-sponsor + YES on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Jan 23 2025, 217-204); YES + co-sponsor of SAVE Act (proof-of-citizenship voter registration); supports physical border barriers 'on land and within waterways' + Remain in Mexico; supports Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity; '100% supportive of the 2A'; opposes counting non-citizens in apportionment; supports parental notification for minor abortions, Hyde, CPC funding; endorsed Trump transgender military ban; opposes Biden Mifepristone pharmacy distribution. PRO-LIFE POSTURE: state-deference framework (Dobbs returns to states), NOT abolitionist — endorses restrictions (Hyde, parental notification, CPC funding) but doesn't advocate constitutional abolition. EMILYs List Women Vote spent against him in 2024. No material cross-party deviations from RSC line.

profile.religion: Protestant — self-identifies 'born-again Christian' (Alaska Watchman Q&A 2024-06-12): 'I was raised in the church... I am a born-again Christian.' Specific church not publicly stated.
profile.background: U.S. Representative AK-At-Large (R), sworn Jan 3, 2025 (119th Congress); defeated incumbent Mary Peltola 51.3-48.7 in 2024 RCV runoff. Baylor BBA + Indiana U Bloomington MBA. Founder, FarShore Partners (software). Grandson of Rep. Nick Begich Sr. (D, lost in 1972 plane crash); nephew of former Sen. Mark Begich (D) — the only Republican in a leading Alaska Democratic dynasty. Married Dharna Vakharia (2002); one son. Lives Chugiak, AK. Committees: Natural Resources (Vice Chair, Energy & Mineral Resources sub), Transportation & Infrastructure (Vice Chair, Railroads/Pipelines sub), Science Space & Technology. Caucuses: Republican Study Committee, Congressional Western Caucus (EVC). Not in House Freedom Caucus.
profile.nra_rating: A (also A from Gun Owners of America)
profile.heritage_action: {'note': 'First term, 119th Congress - score still being compiled'}
profile.office: 153 Cannon House Office Building
profile.confidence_note: US House member bulk-scored via party-default archetype (maga_conservative_r). Per RESOLUTE Citizen methodology, silence on a major v4.0 issue is itself signal — a House member who has not publicly opposed a major issue is scored False on the affirmative-action question. Individual evidence review is pending; when specific votes/statements get promoted via apply-claims.py, this confidence will upgrade to archetype_curated. Run: rescore-house-by-party.py (2026-05-18). 2026-05-18 evidence: applied Iran war powers vote (H.Con.Res.38, 3/5/2026) — voted NAY per roll call source. 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Laken Riley Act (S.5/H.R.29, signed 1/29/2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.21/S.6, Jan 2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on SAVE Act citizenship verification for voter registration (H.R.22, 4/10/2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on NDAA FY26 (HR5371/S1071, 12/10/2025) — $900B defense + $400M Ukraine + Europe troop limits. 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (H.R.28/S.9, Jan/Mar 2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Protect Children's Innocence Act (H.R.3492, 12/17/2025) — federal criminal ban on gender-affirming care for minors. · 2026-05-20 — Default-flagged as incumbent_seeking_reelection (no explicit status).

candidates.name: Mary Peltola
candidates.office: Former US Representative AK-AL · 2026 D U.S. Senate Candidate (challenging Sullivan)
candidates.notes: Former US Rep AK-AL (D, 2022–2025) · NOW 2026 U.S. Senate Candidate (announced Jan 12 2026, challenging Sen. Sullivan). [2026-06 federal autopilot — currency-fix from generic '2026 candidate'] Heritage Action 118th: 18% — roughly 3-4x the typical House Dem; Alaska Public Media (2024): 'one of the least loyal Democrats in the U.S. House.' Score is based on her REAL 118th Congress voting record (one full term). HARD-LEFT on life/marriage/family/parental rights/election integrity (NO Born-Alive, YES Respect for Marriage Act, NO Parents Bill of Rights, NO HR2/Laken Riley, co-sponsor Equality Act, PP 100%). KEY CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (high-value catches): (1) **2A — voted YES to defund Biden's Office of Gun Violence Prevention** (Nov 2023, Rosendale amdt) — one of only TWO House Dems; subsistence-hunter framing; publicly opposed complete assault-weapons ban citing food security. (2) **BORDER — voted YES on End the Border Catastrophe Act (HR 3602, May 2024)** with all Rs present; YES on bill to deport undocumented migrants who assault police. (3) **ISRAEL — voted YES on standalone Israel Security Supplemental** (Apr 20 2024, 366-58). (4) **NDAA — YES on final FY24 NDAA compromise** (Dec 2023, only 6 House Dems). NRA-PVF endorsed her over Begich in 2024 (controversial — actual NRA letter grade was 'D').

r1.title: Voted NO on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (HR 26, Jan 11 2023, RC #29) — voted with all Dems except Cuellar.
r1.excerpt: Voted NO on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (HR 26, Jan 11 2023, RC #29) — voted with all Dems except Cuellar.

r2.title: Voted NO on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (HR 26, Jan 11 2023, RC #29) — voted with all Dems except Cuellar.
r2.excerpt: Voted NO on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (HR 26, Jan 11 2023, RC #29) — voted with all Dems except Cuellar.

r3.title: SBA Pro-Life America scorecard: failing — voted to remove protections for unborn and eliminate Hyde-style funding restrictions.
r3.excerpt: SBA Pro-Life America scorecard: failing — voted to remove protections for unborn and eliminate Hyde-style funding restrictions.

r4.title: Rated 100% by Planned Parenthood Action Fund; co-sponsored Women's Health Protection Act in 118th Congress.
r4.excerpt: Rated 100% by Planned Parenthood Action Fund; co-sponsored Women's Health Protection Act in 118th Congress.

r5.title: Voted NO on House FY24 NDAA specifically citing pro-life amendments (DoD abortion-travel reimbursement block) as reason — opposed life-side restrictions.
r5.excerpt: Voted NO on House FY24 NDAA specifically citing pro-life amendments (DoD abortion-travel reimbursement block) as reason — opposed life-side restrictions.

r6.title: EMILYs List Women Vote ran a major 2024 IE campaign supporting her (most-expensive cycle spend); PP 100% rating reflects abortion-industry alignment.
r6.excerpt: EMILYs List Women Vote ran a major 2024 IE campaign supporting her (most-expensive cycle spend); PP 100% rating reflects abortion-industry alignment.

r7.title: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (HR 8404, Dec 8 2022, 258-169); statement: 'major victory for freedom, privacy, dignity, and equality.'
r7.excerpt: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (HR 8404, Dec 8 2022, 258-169); statement: 'major victory for freedom, privacy, dignity, and equality.'

r8.excerpt: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (HR 8404, Dec 8 2022, 258-169); statement: 'major victory for freedom, privacy, dignity, and equality.'

r9.title: Co-sponsor of HR 15 Equality Act (118th) — adds SOGI to Civil Rights Act with no religious-liberty carve-out.
r9.excerpt: Co-sponsor of HR 15 Equality Act (118th) — adds SOGI to Civil Rights Act with no religious-liberty carve-out.

r10.title: Voted NO on Parents Bill of Rights Act (HR 5, Mar 24 2023, 213-208); stated reason: 'students should have some right to privacy' — i.e., against parental notification on gender/social-transition.

r16.excerpt: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Voted YES on End the Border Catastrophe Act (HR 3602, May 2024) and YES on bill to deport undocumented migrants who assault police officers — joined Republicans.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — competitive district challenger (AK-None). Archetype: establishment_d.
profile.religion: Russian Orthodox (Orthodox Church in Alaska — sizeable AK Native demographic via 19th-c. Russian American mission heritage). Yup'ik name: Akalleq.
profile.background: Former U.S. Rep AK-At-Large (D), Sep 13 2022 – Jan 3 2025. Won 2022 special election (Aug 16 2022) over Sarah Palin via RCV — first Alaska Native ever elected to Congress, first woman to AK's at-large House seat. Lost 2024 re-election to Nick Begich III. ON JAN 12 2026 announced 2026 U.S. Senate campaign challenging incumbent Sen. Dan Sullivan (R). Former ED Kuskokwim River Inter-Tribal Fish Commission; AK state House 1999–2009 (Bethel). Russian Orthodox / Yup'ik. Father Ward Sattler (German-American pilot/teacher); mother Elizabeth 'LizAnn' Williams (Yup'ik). Husband Eugene 'Buzzy' Peltola Jr. died Sept 12 2023 in a Piper Super Cub crash near St. Mary's, AK; 7 children (blended).

candidates.name: Mary Peltola Senate
candidates.office: U.S. Senate Alaska (2026 D Candidate · former U.S. Rep AK-AL · 2022-2024)
candidates.notes: Former U.S. Representative AK-AL (2022-2025). Considering 2026 D U.S. Senate challenge to Dan Sullivan. First Alaska Native member of Congress.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — 2026 AK U.S. Senate challenger. Archetype: establishment_d.

claims.text: Consistently supported abortion rights throughout her 2022–2024 congressional service; earned the endorsement of EMILY's List and stated support for federal abortion access — rejecting any personhood-from-conception standard.

Received an NRA rating of 'D' for supporting universal background checks, mandatory waiting periods for firearm purchases, and raising the minimum purchase age for certain firearms to 21 — positions opposing the rubric's defense of unrestricted Second Amendment rights.

Broke with the Democratic Party in 2024 by voting for border enforcement measures, one of 78 party-line-defying votes during her congressional service; her aisle-crossing votes were concentrated in border and energy policy.

candidates.name: Nancy Dahlstrom
candidates.office: Governor of Alaska (2026 R Candidate · sitting AK Lt Governor · former state rep)
candidates.notes: Sitting AK Lt Governor (since 2022). Former AK state representative. R candidate for AK Governor 2026 (Dunleavy term-limited).

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — 2026 AK Governor race. Archetype: establishment_r. · 2026-05-20 — Deduped batch-suffix record (merged duplicate).

claims.text: Stated as a 2026 Alaska gubernatorial candidate that she would work with the Legislature to place a constitutional amendment before voters clarifying that nothing in the Alaska Constitution creates a right to abortion — affirming a pro-life posture aligned with the rubric's life-at-conception standard.

Longtime member of the National Rifle Association and the Chugiak-Eagle River Friends of the NRA; endorsed by President Donald Trump in her 2024 congressional run; her record reflects consistent opposition to new firearms restrictions — aligning with the rubric's defense of Second Amendment rights.
